_meta.purpose: Fast-load context for the WOW Artwork Engine. Read this first each session to know where we are without re-deriving from code. Keep updated as the project moves.
_meta.productionRepo: wow-contract-query (the Artwork Engine tab lives inside WOW's existing dashboard)

project.name: WOW Artwork Engine
project.oneLiner: Automated weekly AI artwork for WOW Media's billboard network — generate, human-approve, auto-deliver.
project.goal: Every week: generate original AI artwork for the signs, Scott (WOW CEO) reviews/approves on a dashboard, approved videos auto-ship to Jeff (the Projectionist) via Google Drive + email.

people.scott: WOW CEO — reviews and approves artwork on the dashboard
people.jeff: Projectionist — receives approved videos (Google Drive folder + Gmail notification)

status.overall: LIVE IN PRODUCTION. Core pipeline shipped and working. Currently iterating on art direction (motion/3D) and validating the Google delivery handoff.
status.liveSince: 2026-07-11 (Artwork Engine tab on wowautomation.ai / wow-contract-query dashboard)
status.generationMode: live in production (spends real fal.ai money); fixture ($0) locally
status.currentFocus: Motion realism — user wants full-scene active motion on every artwork + real 3D depth baked into the creative prompt (not post-processed letterbox).

milestones.M3_delivery_google_drive_gmail: credentials configured 2026-07-16; test send NOT yet verified end-to-end
milestones.M4_first_live_run: in progress — art direction being tuned before a real weekly send
milestones.M5_scheduler: built but SCHEDULER_ENABLED=false; turn on once art + delivery are signed off

architecture.principle: Deploy INTO WOW's existing AWS infra (same as the Broken News project). NOT a new stack.
architecture.frontend: React tab inside wow-contract-query dashboard (wcq/src/pages/ArtworkEngine.tsx). Standalone dev shell at web/src/App.jsx mirrors it.
architecture.backend: Node/Express in server/. Deployed on EC2 via PM2 (dev + master boxes).
architecture.database: Postgres via pgRepo when DATABASE_URL is set; production currently runs WITHOUT DATABASE_URL, so it uses memoryRepo with JSON snapshot persistence (var/storage/state.json). State survives PM2 restart/deploy.
architecture.storage: Generated media on disk (var/storage) in prod; S3 driver available. Both media and state.json are preserved across 'git reset --hard' deploys.
architecture.deployFlow: push to main (this repo) OR push to wow-contract-query dev -> CI -> staging; PR dev->master + merge -> production. Empty commit triggers redeploy.
architecture.email: Gmail API via Google service account with domain-wide delegation (gmail.send). Sends AS a real @wowmedia.com person (no SMTP).

pipeline.phase1_stills: Seedream v4 text-to-image (~$0.03/still). 9 options per batch = 3 per surface. Cheap style gate — Scott approves before any video spend.
pipeline.phase2_video: Seedance 2.0 image-to-video runs ONLY on approved stills. Standard tier @1080p, then Topaz 2x upscale to 4K-class. Graceful fallback to 1080p if upscale fails.
pipeline.qaGate: Luma readability check on stills for outdoor visibility (blocks <25 or >200 luma; warns 25-55 with 'qa:' prefix, non-blocking).
pipeline.delivery: Approved videos -> Google Drive folder + Gmail notification to Jeff. Offline fallback writes .eml + local files and reports NOT sent.

spectacular.note: WOW signature 3D frame-break look. Currently composites a black canvas + border in ffmpeg (frameBreakComposite). USER FEEDBACK: 3D must be baked into the creative prompt, not a post-processed letterbox the subject can't break through.

eon_connected.note: ONE wide master, subject choreographed to travel across the 3 pods (pod-to-pod illusion). 3-act journey rotates weekly. Billed as ONE Seedance call despite 3 face rows.

config.pingpong: REMOVED 2026-07-16 from spectacular + eon_single. Previously doubled 15s clips to 30s for seamless looping; user found 30s unnecessary. eon_connected never used it (directional travel).

costModel._verified: 2026-07-19 against fal model pages. Decision: keep full 4K quality (Scott/Shawn).
costModel.sharedFalAccount: CRITICAL: the fal account is shared across WOW Artwork + WOW Content Automation + WOW Broken News. fal's billing API (GET api.fal.ai/v1/account/billing?expand=credits and /v1/account/focus) reports ALL THREE COMBINED and cannot isolate artwork. Our per-generation estimate (spend.js) is the ONLY artwork-specific figure. Use fal's account total only as an all-projects ceiling.
costModel.perVideoApprox: 15s at full 4K = Seedance ~$10.20 + Topaz ~$1.20 = ~$11.40 per finished video (NOT the ~$1.50 the old $0.10/sec estimate assumed).
costModel.perFullBatchApprox: 9 stills ($0.27) + videos at ~$11.40 each. One option per surface animated (3 videos) = ~$35. Every option animated = ~$100+.
costModel.history: The earlier dashboard pill under-reported ~7.6x because it used a blended $0.10/sec. Fixed 2026-07-19 to itemized real rates.
costModel.canonicalPriceBook: server/src/services/generation/falPricing.js is now the single source of truth, encoding fal's EXACT token formula (tokens = w*h*dur*24/1024, cost = tokens/1k * rate1k; standard $0.014/1k, fast $0.0112/1k). Pixel-accurate for any aspect (our billboards are 21:9 / 2:3, never 16:9, so flat per-second rates were always wrong). Mirrored verbatim into wow-contract-query/server/modules/pricing/falPricing.js — KEEP IN SYNC.
costModel.immutableLedger: Each artwork row now stores fal_request_id, upscale_request_id, and cost_usd computed at generation time (where both the 1080p Seedance render dims and the 4K final dims are known). EON's one Seedance call is split across its 3 face rows so the ledger sums exactly. spend.js prefers cost_usd; falls back to the canonical estimate for pre-ledger rows. No more retroactive re-pricing.
costModel.crossProjectReconciliation: The fal account is billed by TWO projects: WOW Artwork + WOW Content Automation (NOT Broken News — it's OpenAI/Gemini). Both now use the identical canonical price book. Content Automation's falai.js estimateCost had the Seedance /fast tier at $0.022/s vs the real $0.2419/s (~11x under-report) — FIXED, which auto-corrects its billed_usd. Reconciliation lives in wow-contract-query: GET /api/cost/reconciliation?month= federates the artwork ledger (proxied /api/spend) + content-automation billed_usd + fal FOCUS account total (needs FAL_ADMIN_KEY), and reports attributed vs unattributed. Modules: server/modules/pricing/{falPricing,falAccount,reconcile}.js. Dashboard visual (artifact): https://claude.ai/code/artifact/7fbd1551-702d-44c1-9a60-8e3f4e19c31c
costModel.estimateSource: server/src/services/spend.js — sums the immutable per-generation cost_usd ledger (falls back to the canonical falPricing estimate for old rows). Reconciled against fal's account total (all fal projects) as a ceiling.
billingRules: Itemized: stills (Seedream) + Seedance raw-seconds (rate by tier from model string: 'fast'->720p else 1080p) + Topaz seconds (only when model string contains '+topaz').
Fixture rows (model starts with 'fixture' or no model) are free/excluded.

A motion row bills a Seedance call ONLY if it produced raw output (s3_key_raw set). EON connected = 3 rows sharing one raw key -> billed once. Rows with no raw key are skipped (never completed a billable generation).
Seconds = row's RAW generation length (duration_s). Ping-pong retired 2026-07-16 so duration_s == raw for current runs.
Rows filtered to the current calendar month (created_at, falling back to run week_of).
QA-failed stills still count (generated on Seedream, then gated).
Superseded stills (retired by per-sign regen) STILL count — they were generated and did cost money.

falBillingApi.balance: GET https://api.fal.ai/v1/account/billing?expand=credits  (Authorization: Key <ADMIN_KEY>) -> current_balance USD. Account-wide (all 3 projects).
falBillingApi.focusReport: GET https://api.fal.ai/v1/account/focus?source=invoice|estimate&start=ISO&end=ISO -> FOCUS CSV. Account-wide, 90-day lookback for estimates.

art: Psychedelic, bright, saturated, dynamic — NOT abstract/plain (rejected 'plain silver ball moving left to right').
Characterful creatures as subjects (serpent, koi, octopus, peacock, jellyfish, orchid, butterfly, comet), not generic shapes.
MOTION IS THE POINT: full-scene active motion on every artwork, not subtle. Backgrounds must move too. Subjects travel across the whole screen with 3D depth (in/out, vertical, diagonal).
3D depth must be built into the creative PROMPT so the subject genuinely interacts with spatial planes — do NOT rely on a post-processed black letterbox/frame the subject can't break through.
Showcase the full motion capability of the screens.
ux: Plain, non-technical language for Scott. Button labels must be obvious to a first-time viewer ('Use this design', 'Approve video', 'Pass', 'Try again').
Clear visual feedback during long operations (spinner/overlay while making videos; 'Making videos... a few minutes each').
Per-sign regeneration, not all-or-nothing (regenerate just Spectacular, or just EON single, etc.).
Show month-to-date spend at the top of the dashboard.
Videos long enough to feel substantial (moved 6s -> 15s), but 30s was too long.
process: Wants to operate production via the Claude browser extension / AWS console when possible (drop-in JSON, guided clicks), not always CLI.
Wants durable status/context files so we don't waste tokens re-establishing position each session (this file).
Ship to production readily — 'Scott won't test it until it's there anyways.'

problemsEncountered.fix: strip trailing slashes in both API clients

problemsEncountered.cause: polling race — 202 sent before run.status set to 'running', so dashboard saw stale 'complete'
problemsEncountered.fix: set 'running' BEFORE onStart callback; added spinner overlay + disabled buttons during animation

problemsEncountered.cause: in-memory repo lost all state on process restart
problemsEncountered.fix: JSON snapshot persistence (atomic tmp+rename) in memoryRepo; hydrate on boot; fail interrupted 'running' rows

problemsEncountered.issue: Art too simple ('plain silver ball left to right')
problemsEncountered.fix: characterful creatures + 8 dynamic 3-act choreographies; psychedelic/bright themes
per-surface regenerateStills() route + per-section buttons; unapproved marked 'superseded' and hidden; seed salted per attempt
SPECS bumped to 4K; Seedance 1080p standard + Topaz 2x upscale; frame/inset scale with canvas width

problemsEncountered.issue: Motion extended to 30s + minimal actual motion
problemsEncountered.cause: ping-pong doubling; prompts described subject-only motion which Seedance under-renders
problemsEncountered.fix: removed ping-pong (back to 15s); rewrote prompts to describe whole-SCENE motion (backgrounds churn, every pixel active)

problemsEncountered.cause: relied on ffmpeg-drawn black border/letterbox; subject can't truly break through a post-processed frame
problemsEncountered.fix: moved 3D into the still + motion PROMPTS (foreground/subject/background planes, dark vignette as natural framing); ffmpeg composite still present and may need to be reduced/removed

problemsEncountered.issue: System could not send artwork to team; spend not visible
problemsEncountered.cause: Google creds missing; spend strip not wired
problemsEncountered.fix: spend strip shipped; Google Drive + service-account JSON configured in S3/.env 2026-07-16
problemsEncountered.status: creds in place; end-to-end test send NOT yet verified

openItems: Verify motion/3D visually on a fresh live batch after the 2026-07-16 prompt overhaul.
Decide whether to reduce/remove the ffmpeg frameBreakComposite black border now that 3D is prompt-driven.
Verify Google delivery end-to-end: create batch -> approve -> Send to Jeff (live) -> confirm Drive folder populated + email arrives.

RESOLVED 2026-07-19: the 'over $3K' figure is the shared fal ACCOUNT total (all 3 WOW projects), not artwork. Our spend formula rebuilt to itemized real rates; artwork-only is a fraction. See costModel.
Turn on SCHEDULER_ENABLED once art + delivery are signed off.

why_spend_over_3k.summary: The dashboard number is an ESTIMATE from server/src/services/spend.js, computed from every LIVE generation THIS CALENDAR MONTH — not a real fal.ai bill.
likelyDrivers: All of the project's live art-direction iteration landed in one month (July 2026). Each 'too simple' / 'psychedelic' / '4K' / 'motion' round triggered full live regenerations across all surfaces and options, and each is summed into the single month figure.
Rate was bumped to $0.10/raw-second (4K path) and is applied RETROACTIVELY to all historical videos, including ones actually generated on the old $0.04 720p tier.
Duration grew 6s -> 15s (2.5x more seconds per video, all billed).
Per-sign regenerations pile up: superseded stills still count, and any errored/retried video rows may be counted even if they produced no usable output.

REGRESSION I introduced 2026-07-16: removing ping-pong emptied PINGPONG_STYLES, so historical 30s ping-pong rows (frame_break/eon_single) are now billed at 30s instead of the 15s raw — doubling those.
why_spend_over_3k.howToPinpoint: Hover the spend pill — the tooltip shows 'N designs ($X) + M videos / Ss ($Y)'. If M/S is absurd it's a count/duration bug; if the rate*seconds is the driver it's the retroactive 4K rate. The definitive source is production var/storage/state.json (or the real fal.ai dashboard).
why_spend_over_3k.proposedFix: Make the estimate honest: (a) exclude motion rows that failed with no output, (b) split historical vs current per-second rates, (c) stop retroactively re-pricing old tiers, (d) optionally scope the strip to 'this week' not 'this month'. Confirm against the real fal invoice before trusting either number.

keyFiles.server/src/services/orchestrator.js: runWeek (stills), animateRun (video), regenerateStills (per-sign). Guardrails before spend; QA luma gate; EON slice; frame-break composite.
keyFiles.server/src/services/generation/prompts.j: THEMES, CHOREOGRAPHIES (8 3-act journeys), SOLO_MOTIONS, buildStillPrompt/buildMotionPrompt. Whole-scene motion + 3D-plane language lives here.
keyFiles.server/src/services/generation/catalog.j: SPECS (4K dims), SURFACES, planJobs. Source of truth for dimensions.
keyFiles.server/src/services/generation/fal.js: fal.ai queue logic: Seedream, Seedance 1080p, Topaz upscale with graceful fallback.
keyFiles.server/src/services/ffmpeg.js: frameBreakComposite, pingpong, conform, thumbnail, cropdetect. The black-frame border is drawn here.
keyFiles.server/src/services/spend.js: computeSpend — the MTD estimate behind the $ figure in question.
keyFiles.server/src/services/eonSlicer.js: slices the wide master into 3 faces (derives dims from catalog).
keyFiles.server/src/db/memoryRepo.js: in-memory repo + JSON snapshot persistence (production data store).
keyFiles.KEYS.md: credentials checklist (fal, Google Drive folder, service-account JSON).
